summary.intent: Add a 'brain' reasoning effort toggle to the web UI so users can set Low/Medium/High thinking mode for reasoning-capable models without relying on the awkward @reasoning-* model ID suffix system.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented a self-contained reasoning toggle UI module with model store signals, session restore, i18n strings in three locales, E2E tests, and zero backend changes — all committed and pushed as a PR.

repo: Backend already supports reasoning effort via @reasoning-* model ID suffixes that auto-generate 3 variants per capable model — frontend can piggyback on this without any API changes.
ModelCapabilities includes a `reasoning: bool` flag sent via /api/gon endpoint, making it easy to detect reasoning-capable models on the frontend.
Reasoning-capable models span Anthropic (Claude 3.7+, 4.x), OpenAI (o1/o3/o4/GPT-5), Google (Gemini 2.5+), DeepSeek (R1), and xAI (Grok 3+).
File-size lint checks only apply to Rust source files, not JS — page-chat.js was already 1759 lines before changes.
Biome is used for JS linting/formatting across the web crate.
E2E tests use a base-test.js pattern with WebSocket mocking for chat interactions.
OpenRouter (BYOM) providers were not passing reasoning parameters through — this was the root cause of issue #739.

code.finding: Added reasoningEffort signal (persisted to localStorage), supportsReasoning computed, effectiveModelId computed that appends @reasoning-* suffix, and parseReasoningSuffix/isReasoningVariant helpers.
One-line filter added to exclude @reasoning-* virtual variants from the model dropdown since the toggle replaces them.
New self-contained module with bind/unbind lifecycle, dropdown rendering using existing model-combo/model-dropdown CSS classes, and restoreReasoningFromModelId() session helper.
restoreSessionState() updated to call restoreReasoningFromModelId() to handle legacy @reasoning-* suffixes in stored session model IDs.

workflow: Tailwind CSS rebuild was attempted but the build tooling path was unclear — ultimately determined unnecessary since only existing CSS classes were reused.
Running Rust compilation check (cargo build) after JS-only changes is a useful sanity check to confirm no Rust files were accidentally touched.
Staging files required navigating to repo root since the working directory was a worktree subdirectory.

friction: Tailwind CSS rebuild path was unclear — multiple attempts with different paths failed before determining the rebuild was unnecessary.
Working directory was a worktree path, requiring explicit navigation to repo root for git staging commands.
No Chinese (zh) locale file was initially found via Glob, requiring a fallback Read after discovering the path.
open_items: OpenRouter and other OpenAI-compatible BYOM providers still need to actually pass reasoning_effort through their provider implementation — the UI toggle works but OpenRouter backend support for #739 may still be incomplete.

The @reasoning-* suffix variants are still generated by the backend and sent in the model list — they're just filtered on the frontend. Consider removing backend variant generation once the toggle is the canonical UX.
No CSS was added for the reasoning toggle — it reuses model-combo classes which may not be a perfect visual fit; may need design review.
E2E tests were written but not executed in CI during this session — need to verify they pass in the full test suite.
